refined_fr.refined_fr: CR-NGHE-003.1 (Energy Efficiency Monitoring and Reporting): The NGHE solution shall continuously monitor energy consumption and work output, calculate energy_per_ton, and report this metric against the customer-defined energy efficiency target.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR 'support achieving' is too vague for direct verification. This refinement breaks it down into measurable actions: monitoring, calculation, and reporting, which are necessary precursors to 'achieving' the target. It also clarifies the specific metric (energy_per_ton) and the comparison against a target.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verify that the system accurately calculates energy_per_ton based on measured energy consumption and work output, and that it correctly reports whether this metric meets the defined target over specified periods.
sub_frs: CR-NGHE-003.1.1 (Energy Consumption Monitoring): The NGHE solution shall monitor and record the energy consumption of all relevant subsystems.
CR-NGHE-003.1.2 (Work Output Monitoring): The NGHE solution shall monitor and record the work output (e.g., tons moved, material processed) of the equipment.
CR-NGHE-003.1.3 (Energy Efficiency Calculation): The NGHE solution shall calculate energy_per_ton based on monitored energy consumption and work output.
CR-NGHE-003.1.4 (Target Comparison and Reporting): The NGHE solution shall compare the calculated energy_per_ton against the customer-defined target and report the status to relevant stakeholders.

candidate_dps: Implement a dedicated Energy Management System (EMS) that integrates with vehicle sensors and operational data to calculate and report energy efficiency.
Utilize existing vehicle control units to log energy and work data, then offload for post-processing and reporting via a separate analytics platform.
Develop a distributed sensor network for energy and work monitoring, with local processing and aggregated reporting to a central dashboard.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-NGHE-003.1 (Integrated Energy Management System): An integrated Energy Management System (EMS) shall be developed to collect real-time energy consumption data from vehicle power systems and work output data from payload/material sensors. The EMS shall perform continuous calculation of energy_per_ton and provide a dashboard for target comparison and reporting.

coupling_check.notes: This DP focuses on a single, integrated system for monitoring, calculation, and reporting, which inherently reduces coupling by centralizing the logic. It interacts with vehicle sensors but the core logic is self-contained.

assumptions_risks: Assumption: Accurate and reliable energy consumption sensors are available on all relevant vehicle subsystems.
Assumption: Accurate and reliable work output sensors (e.g., payload scales, material flow sensors) are available and integrated.
Risk: Integration complexity with diverse vehicle sensor types and data formats could be high.
Risk: Real-time processing demands for continuous calculation might exceed available computational resources on the vehicle.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The overarching system responsible for integrating all sub-solutions to meet mission objectives.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Reporting energy_per_ton and target status to external stakeholders/systems.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Communication channel for data collection from vehicle sensors and reporting.

module_containment_scores.notes: High containment as the EMS is a core capability of the overall solution. Low diffusion as it's a central component.

Partial containment. The EMS needs to reside on the vehicle to collect real-time data, but the reporting and overall management might extend beyond the vehicle itself. High diffusion if parts of the EMS logic are spread across vehicle ECUs.
Low containment. Teleoperation might consume the energy efficiency data but is not primarily responsible for its generation or calculation.
Low containment. Safety Services might use energy data for operational safety limits but is not the primary owner of energy efficiency calculation.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The Integrated Energy Management System (EMS) is a core capability of the overall NGHE Solution, integrating data from various vehicle subsystems and providing a unified view of energy efficiency. While components of the EMS will reside on the Vehicle (e.g., data acquisition), the overall system logic, calculation, and reporting are best owned by the NGHE Solution to ensure consistency and provide a holistic view across the fleet.
containment_risks: Risk: If the EMS is too tightly coupled to specific vehicle hardware, it might hinder future upgrades or integration with new vehicle types.
Risk: Distributed data collection and processing across vehicle components could lead to data synchronization issues or inconsistencies if not managed centrally by the NGHE Solution.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DP (Integrated Energy Management System) directly addresses all aspects of the refined FR (monitoring, calculation, reporting of energy_per_ton against target). It provides a clear 'how' for the 'what'.

diffusion_risks: Potential for diffusion if the 'Integrated EMS' is interpreted as a collection of disparate modules rather than a cohesive system owned by NGHE Solution. This could lead to redundant calculation logic or inconsistent reporting across different vehicle types.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The 'NGHE Solution' module is a suitable container for the overall EMS logic, even if parts of its data acquisition reside within the 'Vehicle' module. This allows for centralized management and reporting.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification of the refined FR (monitoring, calculation, reporting) is feasible against the 'NGHE Solution' IM. The IM provides the necessary interfaces (Telemetry, Network Link) to observe its behavior and outputs.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation is clear: the NGHE Solution owns the end-to-ton energy efficiency monitoring and reporting capability. The Vehicle module provides the raw data, but the intelligence resides in the NGHE Solution.

decision.justification: The current level of decomposition has successfully refined the FR, selected a suitable DP, and assigned it to an appropriate IM. However, the refined FR (CR-NGHE-003.1) is not yet atomic, as indicated by its sub-FRs. Further decomposition is required to detail the individual monitoring, calculation, and reporting functions.
next_level_sub_frs: CR-NGHE-003.1.1 (Energy Consumption Monitoring): The NGHE solution shall monitor and record the energy consumption of all relevant subsystems.
CR-NGHE-003.1.2 (Work Output Monitoring): The NGHE solution shall monitor and record the work output (e.g., tons moved, material processed) of the equipment.
CR-NGHE-003.1.3 (Energy Efficiency Calculation): The NGHE solution shall calculate energy_per_ton based on monitored energy consumption and work output.
CR-NGHE-003.1.4 (Target Comparison and Reporting): The NGHE solution shall compare the calculated energy_per_ton against the customer-defined target and report the status to relevant stakeholders.

assumptions: Accurate and reliable energy consumption sensors are available on all relevant vehicle subsystems.
Accurate and reliable work output sensors (e.g., payload scales, material flow sensors) are available and integrated.
open_risks: Integration complexity with diverse vehicle sensor types and data formats could be high.
Real-time processing demands for continuous calculation might exceed available computational resources on the vehicle.
If the EMS is too tightly coupled to specific vehicle hardware, it might hinder future upgrades or integration with new vehicle types.
Distributed data collection and processing across vehicle components could lead to data synchronization issues or inconsistencies if not managed centrally by the NGHE Solution.
evidence_needed: Sensor specifications and availability for energy consumption and work output.
Analysis of computational resources required for real-time energy efficiency calculation.
Interface control documents (ICDs) for vehicle data integration.
